core_payload.content:
# N-Infinity Governance Metrics

## 1. Boundary
N-Infinity Governance Metrics owns the abstract structures the swarm uses to reason about graph quality, epistemic tension, and relation semantics. This sub-hub exists so those metrics and reasoning abstractions remain explicit instead of being mixed into runtime substrate or worker-roster descriptions.

## 2. What Belongs Here
This sub-hub groups `capsuleos.relation-types`, `capsuleos.confidence-vector`, and `epistemic-ledger`. Together they define the typed graph edges the swarm traverses, the confidence geometry it uses to prioritize work, and the contradiction memory it uses to preserve reasoning history.

## 3. What Does Not Belong Here
This sub-hub does not own the runtime substrate that hosts work, the swarm workers that carry it out, or the external lanes that feed the swarm fresh signal. Those belong to `runtime-fabric`, `swarm-roster`, and `signal-fabric`.

neuro_concentrate.summary: N-Infinity Governance Metrics groups the reasoning abstractions the swarm uses to interpret graph quality: relation types, confidence vector, and epistemic ledger. It keeps semantic law and epistemic memory distinct from runtime substrate, incoming signals, and worker roles. This matters because the swarm needs a visible boundary for relation semantics, confidence interpretation, and memory of epistemic events so quality judgment does not disappear into worker folklore or runtime machinery over time.

epistemic_ledger.rationale: Minted as part of n-infinity hub decomposition to reduce parent link density from 26 outbound to ≤9.
